changes: Added platform-specific stub: `install_hooks_linux()` for future implementation
Updated `Command::InstallHooks` handler to dispatch to platform-specific implementations
Used conditional compilation (#[cfg(target_os = ...)]) for platform gating
file_insights.complexity: medium - path resolution, XML parsing, launchctl integration

patterns_discovered.description: Multiple fallback strategies to resolve repo root: canonicalize() with unwrap_or(), parent() checks, and explicit .cards directory detection
Scans .cards/ directory for team-* subdirectories to automatically populate WatchPaths in plist, enabling multi-team support without hardcoding
Uses string find/replace operations to modify plist content: placeholder replacement and WatchPaths array injection
Uses #[cfg(target_os = ...)] attributes for macOS/Linux branching with unsupported platform error message
Uses std::process::Command to invoke launchctl with output capture and status checking, logs warning if load fails

gotchas_discovered.description: canonicalize() may fail in test/temp directories; code uses unwrap_or_else with fallback to preserve original path
Parsing plist with substring find/replace could break if template structure changes (e.g., newlines, spacing, key order). No XML parser used.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Template format is fixed; brittle but works if template structure stable

gotchas_discovered.description: Manual substring splicing to inject WatchPaths XML: finds <array> tag after WatchPaths key, replaces content up to </array>. Assumes specific template structure.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Works for current template; would need refactoring if template evolves

gotchas_discovered.description: Uses current_exe() to determine bop binary path, replaces placeholder in plist. If binary moves, plist becomes stale.
launchctl load failure is logged as warning, not error; installation proceeds. User may not notice if loading fails.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Stderr message printed; user can verify with `launchctl list sh.bop.merge-gate`

gotchas_discovered.description: Uses into_string() on OsString without error handling in team-* discovery; silently skips directories with non-UTF8 names
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Acceptable for typical team-* naming; non-UTF8 names rare

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully implemented macOS installation logic that registers a launchctl plist for the bop merge-gate daemon. Implementation handles multi-team setups through dynamic WatchPaths discovery, includes proper error context, and establishes platform-specific patterns for future Linux systemd support.
key_achievements: Dynamic team directory discovery for WatchPaths - no hardcoding needed
Robust path resolution with fallbacks for various deployment scenarios
LaunchAgent registration with -w flag for persistent loading
Platform-gated compilation with error for unsupported platforms
approach_outcome.scope_completed: Full macOS installation logic; Linux stub prepared for future implementation

recommendations.recommendation: Replace string-based plist manipulation with xml-rs or plist crate
recommendations.rationale: Current substring-based parsing is fragile if template structure changes. Proper XML parsing would be more maintainable.
recommendations.effort: medium - would require adding dependency and refactoring template replacement logic

recommendations.recommendation: Add launchctl load failure as hard error instead of warning
recommendations.rationale: If launchctl load fails, the daemon never starts. Silent-fail is confusing for users.
recommendations.effort: low - change return type to Result and propagate error

recommendations.recommendation: Implement Linux systemd installation (stub currently present)
recommendations.rationale: Linux support needed for full cross-platform daemon deployment
recommendations.effort: high - requires systemd user service understanding and testing

recommendations.recommendation: Add integration test for macOS installation logic
recommendations.rationale: Template path resolution, plist generation, and launchctl invocation are difficult to test in CI without macOS runner
recommendations.effort: medium - would need macOS-specific test harness

recommendations.recommendation: Validate plist output before loading with launchctl
recommendations.rationale: Catch malformed plist early rather than relying on launchctl to reject it
recommendations.effort: low - use plist crate to validate before write
